critical_issues: In Section 6.3 (Bookings page inventory), Booking Detail lists Feature IDs as 'F-005–F-009' which incorrectly includes F-008 (Booking Calendar View). F-008 belongs to the Bookings List page (#5), not Booking Detail (#6). The Phase 6B page inventory correctly lists Booking Detail as F-005, F-006, F-007, F-009 (excluding F-008). Since Section 6 is described as 'the definitive frontend build map', this could cause Phase 7 to misscope a build card for F-008 onto the wrong page.
contradictions: D55-P6B-004 (Network Section decision: 'Network section houses Referral Partners at launch and absorbs structured Vendor profiles when F-029 ships in Growth') is omitted from the Phase 6B Decision Records summary in Section 18. The entries jump from D55-P6B-003 directly to D55-P6B-005, listing only 24 of the claimed 25 Phase 6B entries.

The cross-reference manifest's authenticated_routes list omits two Growth-tier routes (/network?tab=vendors and /network/vendors/[id]) that ARE present in the manifest's pages export array, creating an inconsistency within the same manifest.

The transition timing token names changed between Phase 6A (--transition-fast, --transition-medium, --transition-slow at 150ms/200ms/300ms) and the consolidated D7 Section 2.4 (--transition-micro, --transition-state, --transition-page, --transition-complex at 100-600ms ranges) without explicit acknowledgment of the rename or a divergence note per the D3 precedent.
warnings: The cross-reference manifest pages export array contains 34 page entries but page_count is stated as 39. The 5 system pages (404, 500, 403, Maintenance, Session Expired) are documented in Section 6.9 but absent from the manifest's pages array, which could confuse downstream consumers expecting the manifest to be exhaustive.

The Tailwind v4 @theme configuration in Section 2.5 defines color and font tokens but does not include the transition timing tokens from Section 2.4. Developers would need to apply transition values as ad-hoc Tailwind utility classes rather than consuming a single @theme source of truth, reducing the governance benefit described in Section 17 Rule 2 (Token Discipline).

D14 Section 2.1 shows Gold-500 on Navy-800 at exactly 4.5:1, which passes WCAG AA by the absolute minimum margin. Any slight rendering variation could push this below threshold. Consider documenting this borderline pairing as a monitored risk or recommending gold-400/gold-300 for text on navy-800 to provide headroom.

Section 12.6 (Keyboard Shortcuts) defines only Cmd+K for search and defers all others to Growth, but Section 11.1 also references Cmd+K. No conflict, but the keyboard shortcut reference modal mentioned ('accessible via ? key opening a reference modal') would display only one shortcut at MVP, which may feel incomplete — consider noting this is intentional.
